title: Container Start fails using compat API
body:
<!--
---------------------------------------------------
BUG REPORT INFORMATION
---------------------------------------------------
Use the commands below to provide key information from your environment:
You do NOT have to include this information if this is a FEATURE REQUEST

**NOTE** A large number of issues reported against Podman are often found to already be fixed
in more current versions of the project.  Before reporting an issue, please verify the
version you are running with `podman version` and compare it to the latest release
documented on the top of Podman's [README.md](../README.md).  If they differ, please
update your version of Podman to the latest possible and retry your command before creating
an issue.

Also, there is a running list of known issues in the [Podman Troubleshooting Guide](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/troubleshooting.md),
please reference that page before opening a new issue.

If you are filing a bug against `podman build`, please instead file a bug
against Buildah (https://github.com/containers/buildah/issues). Podman build
executes Buildah to perform container builds, and as such the Buildah
maintainers are best equipped to handle these bugs.
-->

**Is this a BUG REPORT or FEATURE REQUEST? (leave only one on its own line)**

/kind bug

**Description**

<!--
Briefly describe the problem you are having in a few paragraphs.
-->

When creating a container using the docker client pointed to the podman socket started with `/usr/bin/podman system service --time=0 unix:///home/kwiesmueller/docker.socket` the container can not be started, but returns an error:
```
{
  "cause": "OCI runtime error",
  "message": "mount `/dev/stdout` to '/dev/stdout': Invalid argument: OCI runtime error",
  "response": 500
}
```

**Steps to reproduce the issue:**

1. Running on a fresh Fedora 33.
2. Start the podman socket
`/usr/bin/podman system service --time=0 unix:///home/$(whoami)/docker.socket`
3. Send a container create request as it's being sent by the docker client:
```
POST http://unix:/home/kwiesmueller/docker.socket:/d/v1.24/containers/create?name=kubevirt-dnsmasq2
{
    "Hostname": "",
    "Domainname": "",
    "User": "",
    "AttachStdin": false,
    "AttachStdout": false,
    "AttachStderr": false,
    "ExposedPorts": {
        "2201/tcp": {},
        "443/tcp": {},
        "5000/tcp": {},
        "5901/tcp": {},
        "6443/tcp": {},
        "80/tcp": {},
        "8443/tcp": {}
    },
    "Tty": false,
    "OpenStdin": false,
    "StdinOnce": false,
    "Env": [
        "NUM_NODES=1",
        "NUM_SECONDARY_NICS=0"
    ],
    "Cmd": [
        "/bin/bash",
        "-c",
        "/dnsmasq.sh"
    ],
    "Image": "kubevirtci/k8s-1.19",
    "Volumes": null,
    "WorkingDir": "",
    "Entrypoint": null,
    "OnBuild": null,
    "Labels": null,
    "HostConfig": {
        "Binds": null,
        "ContainerIDFile": "",
        "LogConfig": {
            "Type": "",
            "Config": null
        },
        "NetworkMode": "",
        "PortBindings": {},
        "RestartPolicy": {
            "Name": "",
            "MaximumRetryCount": 0
        },
        "AutoRemove": false,
        "VolumeDriver": "",
        "VolumesFrom": null,
        "CapAdd": null,
        "CapDrop": null,
        "Dns": null,
        "DnsOptions": null,
        "DnsSearch": null,
        "ExtraHosts": [
            "nfs:192.168.66.2",
            "registry:192.168.66.2",
            "ceph:192.168.66.2"
        ],
        "GroupAdd": null,
        "IpcMode": "",
        "Cgroup": "",
        "Links": null,
        "OomScoreAdj": 0,
        "PidMode": "",
        "Privileged": true,
        "PublishAllPorts": true,
        "ReadonlyRootfs": false,
        "SecurityOpt": null,
        "UTSMode": "",
        "UsernsMode": "",
        "ShmSize": 0,
        "ConsoleSize": [
            0,
            0
        ],
        "Isolation": "",
        "CpuShares": 0,
        "Memory": 0,
        "NanoCpus": 0,
        "CgroupParent": "",
        "BlkioWeight": 0,
        "BlkioWeightDevice": null,
        "BlkioDeviceReadBps": null,
        "BlkioDeviceWriteBps": null,
        "BlkioDeviceReadIOps": null,
        "BlkioDeviceWriteIOps": null,
        "CpuPeriod": 0,
        "CpuQuota": 0,
        "CpuRealtimePeriod": 0,
        "CpuRealtimeRuntime": 0,
        "CpusetCpus": "",
        "CpusetMems": "",
        "Devices": null,
        "DiskQuota": 0,
        "KernelMemory": 0,
        "MemoryReservation": 0,
        "MemorySwap": 0,
        "MemorySwappiness": null,
        "OomKillDisable": null,
        "PidsLimit": 0,
        "Ulimits": null,
        "CpuCount": 0,
        "CpuPercent": 0,
        "IOMaximumIOps": 0,
        "IOMaximumBandwidth": 0,
        "Mounts": [
            {
                "Type": "bind",
                "Source": "/lib/modules",
                "Target": "/lib/modules"
            }
        ]
    },
    "NetworkingConfig": null
}
```
4. Send the start command with the returned ID
```
POST http://unix:/home/kwiesmueller/docker.socket:/v1.24/containers/317c1bb9ac0d5797172c6ec12aa1d48b728bf4fcc7c7b72c3c6146b00db5438d/start
```
**Describe the results you received:**
The call to `/start` has the following body (Status 500):
```
{
  "cause": "OCI runtime error",
  "message": "mount `/dev/stdout` to '/dev/stdout': Invalid argument: OCI runtime error",
  "response": 500
}
```

**Describe the results you expected:**
```
The container to be started.
```


**Additional information you deem important (e.g. issue happens only occasionally):**

**Output of `podman version`:**

```
Version:      2.1.1
API Version:  2.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.2
Built:        Wed Oct  7 18:21:20 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

**Output of `podman info --debug`:**

```
(paste your output here)
```

**Package info (e.g. output of `rpm -q podman` or `apt list podman`):**

```

  slirp4netns:

  swapFree: 4294963200
```

**Have you tested with the latest version of Podman and have you checked the Podman Troubleshooting Guide?**

No and yes.

**Additional environment details (AWS, VirtualBox, physical, etc.):**
Fresh Fedora 33 Workstation on Lenovo Laptop.

comments.body:
After trying with
```
Version:      2.2.0
API Version:  2.1.0
Go Version:   go1.15.5
Built:        Tue Dec  1 18:04:57 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```
The situation changes a little and the create request fails with: 
```
{
  "cause": "incorrect mount format: should be --mount type=<bind|tmpfs|volume>,[src=<host-dir|volume-name>,]target=<ctr-dir>[,options]",
  "message": "fill out specgen: incorrect mount format: should be --mount type=<bind|tmpfs|volume>,[src=<host-dir|volume-name>,]target=<ctr-dir>[,options]",
  "response": 500
}
```

Could it be this line?
https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/v2.2/cmd/podman/common/create_opts.go#L209
It adds a dest to the mount instead of target.

@kwiesmueller I've added a [test](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8668) for this reported issue and it works on master. I marked the [test](https://github.com/containers/podman/pull/8668/files#diff-8b4650a6fccff9bbe88e2a6ecc922307ba49ad5cf0eb98e5a4a1e2122622e562R63) to be skipped because of the size of the image.

I have successfully run the test as root and rootless.

```shell
bin/podman version
Version:      3.0.0-dev
API Version:  3.0.0
Go Version:   go1.15.5
Git Commit:   ef6748dc87126765cc97b6901d0f5adf297914d4-dirty
Built:        Mon Dec  7 15:18:07 2020
OS/Arch:      linux/amd64
```

@jwhonce just closed my PR for this, it seems to work on master.
Do you know if both options (dst and target) are documented somewhere?
Just to make sure this remains stable.

Thanks!
It looks like the manpages for `podman run` and `podman create` need to be updated

They currently say?                    · dst, destination, target: mount destination spec.
Are all three supported? dst, destination and target?
That does look correct, actually. If it says that we're probably fine.
